text: Entering this modern, almost clinical space, I am enveloped by an atmosphere that feels both intriguing and slightly unsettling. The harsh lines and glass panels place me in a liminal zone—somewhere between openness and enclosure. I sense an invitation to knowledge, as if I am standing on the threshold of discovery, yet also aware of the artificiality and sterility that pervades such spaces. My curiosity is awake, yet my presence feels ephemeral, like a visitor to a world in constant flux.
